title: SACRAMENTO – Renters, developers and housing advocates joined State Senator Dave Cortese (D-Silicon Valley) during a zoom news conference today to voice their support for his bill

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 750 aims to change the structure of housing finance in California by using public credit to increase the state’s housing supply faster and decrease the high cost of rentals.",
    "The proposed bill will allow California to potentially draw in investors to unlock hundreds of thousands of shovel-ready homes by using the full faith and credit of the state to back financing for housing projects.",
    "The California Housing Finance and Credit Act would lower capital costs, reduce financing layers, and de-risk larger projects, unlocking both public and private investment for affordable housing.",
    "The financing structure proposed in SB 750 is expected to decrease the high rental costs currently plaguing California.",
    "The California Housing and Credit Act promises to draw billions in private investment for producing the housing California needs.",
    "By insuring loans and municipal bonds, the California Housing and Credit Act aims to unstick shovel-ready projects, ramp up multifamily construction, and drive down rental costs for Californians.",
    "SB 750 would allow the state to use its own credit to guarantee financing for multifamily housing, enabling builders to access better loan terms and move projects forward faster without costing taxpayers from the General Fund.",
    "SB 750 is designed to reduce the financial risk that builders face by using the state’s ability to temporarily insure their loans and thereby lower costs."
  ]
}
```
